audit_items.proof_trace: All functions in lockf.c take an `int fd` parameter — no pointer parameters exist. The only pointer-typed values in the file are `&file_lock` (address of a stack-allocated `struct flock` in the USE_FCNTL variant) and arguments to system calls, both of which are unconditionally non-NULL by C semantics. No heap allocation and no pointer dereferences through pointer variables appear anywhere in the file, so the null-pointer-deref property holds across all four compile-time variants.
